tools.name: click
tools.description: Clicks on the provided element

tools.name: close_page
tools.description: Closes the page by its index. The last open page cannot be closed.

tools.name: drag
tools.description: Drag an element onto another element

tools.name: emulate
tools.description: Emulates various features on the selected page.

tools.name: evaluate_script
tools.description:
Evaluate a JavaScript function inside the currently selected page. Returns the response as JSON
so returned values have to JSON-serializable.

tools.name: fill
tools.description: Type text into a input, text area or select an option from a <select> element.

tools.name: fill_form
tools.description: Fill out multiple form elements at once

tools.name: get_console_message
tools.description: Gets a console message by its ID. You can get all messages by calling list_console_messages.

tools.name: get_network_request
tools.description: Gets a network request by an optional reqid, if omitted returns the currently selected request in the DevTools Network panel.

tools.name: handle_dialog
tools.description: If a browser dialog was opened, use this command to handle it

tools.name: hover
tools.description: Hover over the provided element

tools.name: list_console_messages
tools.description: List all console messages for the currently selected page since the last navigation.

tools.name: list_network_requests
tools.description: List all requests for the currently selected page since the last navigation.

tools.name: list_pages
tools.description: Get a list of pages open in the browser.

tools.name: navigate_page
tools.description: Navigates the currently selected page to a URL.

tools.name: new_page
tools.description: Creates a new page

tools.name: performance_analyze_insight
tools.description: Provides more detailed information on a specific Performance Insight of an insight set that was highlighted in the results of a trace recording.

tools.name: performance_start_trace
tools.description: Starts a performance trace recording on the selected page. This can be used to look for performance problems and insights to improve the performance of the page. It will also report Core Web Vital (CWV) scores for the page.

tools.name: performance_stop_trace
tools.description: Stops the active performance trace recording on the selected page.

tools.name: press_key
tools.description: Press a key or key combination. Use this when other input methods like fill() cannot be used (e.g., keyboard shortcuts, navigation keys, or special key combinations).

tools.name: resize_page
tools.description: Resizes the selected page's window so that the page has specified dimension

tools.name: select_page
tools.description: Select a page as a context for future tool calls.

tools.name: take_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the page or element.

tools.name: take_snapshot
tools.description:
Take a text snapshot of the currently selected page based on the a11y tree. The snapshot lists page elements along with a unique
identifier (uid). Always use the latest snapshot. Prefer taking a snapshot over taking a screenshot. The snapshot indicates the element selected
in the DevTools Elements panel (if any).

tools.name: upload_file
tools.description: Upload a file through a provided element.

tools.name: wait_for
tools.description: Wait for the specified text to appear on the selected page.

resources.description: Unified server manifest with runtime files and API endpoints

resources.description: Unified server manifest with runtime files and API endpoints

resources.description: Unified server manifest with runtime files and API endpoints

resources.description: Unified server manifest with runtime files and API endpoints

resources.description: Unified server manifest with runtime files and API endpoints

resources.name: juris_Juris Components
resources.description: Component registry with all available components

resources.name: juris_Juris Labels
resources.description: Text substitution dictionary for Juris component labels

tools.name: tailwind_generate_css
tools.description: Generate CSS for HTML content containing Tailwind classes

tools.name: svg_list_svg_icons
tools.description: List available SVG icons from multiple icon families with their variants

tools.name: svg_get_svg_icon
tools.description: Get SVG markup for a specific icon from multiple icon families

tools.name: svg_get_svg_stats
tools.description: Get comprehensive statistics about available icon families and the overall collection

tools.name: svg_get_random_svg_icons
tools.description: Get random icons from icon families, useful for sampling or discovery

tools.name: svg_get_svg_icons_batch
tools.description: Get multiple SVG icons in a single request. More efficient than multiple get_svg_icon calls when fetching many icons.

tools.name: stencil_generate_components
tools.description: Generate Stencil web components from YAML specification

tools.name: stencil_validate_yaml
tools.description: Validate YAML specification format

tools.name: lit_generate_components
tools.description: Generate Lit web components from YAML specification

tools.name: lit_validate_yaml
tools.description: Validate YAML specification format

tools.name: juris_generate_preview
tools.description: Generate live preview page for Juris.js component from YAML specification

tools.name: juris_validate_yaml
tools.description: Validate YAML specification format

resources.name: eventing_get_events_summary
resources.description:
High-level overview of all event activity.

Provides pre-computed statistics and insights to save tokens.
Claude can reference this with @event://summary for quick overview.

This is a static resource (URI never changes) with dynamic content
(updated based on current server state).

resources.name: eventing_get_events_help
resources.description:
Documentation for the eventing system.

Explains the difference between tools and resources:
- Tools: Actions (wait, clear, delete) and raw data access
- Resources: Pre-computed summaries for token efficiency

templates.name: eventing_get_session_summary
templates.description:
Session-specific summary resource template.

Provides pre-computed statistics for a single session including:
- Event count
- Last activity timestamp
- Event type breakdown
- Session status (active/idle)

This is more token-efficient than calling get_events() when you
only need summary statistics rather than full event data.

Example URIs:
- event://session/abc-123
- event://session/test-session
- event://session/gallery-xyz

templates.name: eventing_get_session_latest
templates.description:
Get the most recent event for a session.

Returns the latest event data without fetching all events.
Most token-efficient option when you only need the newest event.

Use cases:
- "What was the last action in this session?"
- "Show me the most recent event"
- Polling for new activity without fetching full history

Example URIs:
- event://session/abc-123/latest
- event://session/test-session/latest
- event://session/gallery-xyz/latest

tools.name: gallery_generate_gallery
tools.description:
Generate an SVG icon gallery and optionally serve it.

This tool orchestrates the three-step gallery workflow:
1. Fetch icons from svg-mcp-server
2. Render gallery.html with htpy renderer (interactive with clipboard, toasts, webhooks)
3. Optionally start HTTP server on configurable port (default: 3042)

The gallery includes:
- Click-to-copy icon names to clipboard
- Toast notifications for user feedback
- Event bus integration (mitt.js)
- Webhook integration with eventing-mcp (events sent to localhost:3100)

Returns metadata including URL, session ID, and generation status.

tools.name: eventing_wait_for_event
tools.description:
Block until an event arrives for the specified session, or timeout.

This is the primary tool for interactive workflows:
1. Generate a UI with a session_id
2. User interacts with UI (triggers webhook)
3. Call wait_for_event(session_id) to retrieve the data

Returns event data if received, or timeout indicator if no event arrives.

tools.name: eventing_get_events
tools.description:
Get events for a session, optionally filtered by timestamp.

Non-blocking - returns immediately with matching events.
Supports pagination via limit/offset for large result sets.

Pagination examples:
- Page 1: get_events(session_id, limit=50, offset=0)
- Page 2: get_events(session_id, limit=50, offset=50)
- Page 3: get_events(session_id, limit=50, offset=100)

The response includes pagination metadata to help navigate large datasets:
- total_count: Total number of events matching filters
- has_more: Whether more events exist beyond this page
- next_offset: Offset value for the next page (if has_more is true)

tools.name: eventing_clear_events
tools.description:
Clear all events for a session.

Useful for resetting state between tests or cleaning up after workflow completion.

tools.name: eventing_delete_session
tools.description:
Fully remove a session from memory.

Deletes the session entirely from both queues and history.
Use when workflow is finished and session is no longer needed.
This frees memory completely, unlike clear_events which keeps the session structure.

tools.name: eventing_list_sessions
tools.description:
List all sessions with metadata.

Returns information about all active sessions including:
- session_id: Unique session identifier
- event_count: Number of events stored
- last_activity: Timestamp of most recent event
- has_queue: Whether session has an active queue
- queue_size: Number of pending events in queue

Useful for identifying orphaned sessions that need cleanup.

tools.name: eventing_cleanup_empty_sessions
tools.description:
Remove all sessions with zero events.

Batch cleanup of empty sessions to free memory.
Returns count of sessions removed.
Useful for periodic maintenance.

tools.name: eventing_get_all_events
tools.description:
Get events for all sessions, grouped by session.

Returns all events across all sessions in a structured format:
- total_sessions: Total number of sessions with events
- total_events: Total event count across all sessions
- sessions: Dictionary of session_id -> {event_count, last_activity, events[]}

This is more efficient than calling list_sessions() followed by get_events()
for each session when you need all event data.

tools.name: eventing_clear_all_events
tools.description:
Clear all events for all sessions.

Removes event history from all sessions but keeps the session structures intact.
Useful for resetting state across the entire system while maintaining sessions.

Returns:
- total_cleared: Total number of events cleared
- sessions_affected: Number of sessions that had events cleared
- session_counts: Dictionary of session_id -> events_cleared_count

tools.name: eventing_ping
tools.description:
Ping the eventing server to check for new events.

This tool checks if there are any events in the EventBus and sends
a notification if the event://summary resource has changed. This enables
a hybrid pull-push pattern where Claude polls via this tool, but receives
notifications about resource updates.

Pattern:
1. Claude calls ping() periodically
2. If new events exist, ping() sends resource notification
3. Claude can then re-read event://summary for updated data

This works because notifications are sent INSIDE the request context
where ctx.request_context.session is valid.

Returns:
- status: "ok"
- total_events: Number of events in system
- notification_sent: Whether a notification was sent

tools.name: eventing_delete_all_sessions
tools.description:
Delete all sessions from memory.

Completely removes all sessions, freeing all memory.
This is a destructive operation that clears both event history and session queues.
Use at end of testing or when you want a complete reset.

Returns:
- deleted_count: Number of sessions deleted
- session_ids: List of session IDs that were deleted
